notes: Gravity for Nicola's Friday night. The boat at brunch volume, the imperious wave from Table 12, the bell above the door, the bus kid making the salad station look bad, the call from the helm. Endgame cards bring Dean to Table 14 and the second voice into Nicola's interior. Grounded reality: the kicks Nicola is feeling are the third's; the static-bloom is a metaphor she has in her head. No animism. The boat is not, today, alive.

deck.title: the bell rings
deck.flavor: above the front door · the way it rang in 1962 · a party of six has arrived twenty minutes early.

deck.title: table 12 waves
deck.flavor: the imperious wave. you finish what you are doing. you do not, on a friday night, run for table 12.

deck.title: the pass calls
deck.flavor: hector through the gap. plates are up. the heat lamp is at the level the heat lamp is at.

deck.title: the first kick this hour
deck.flavor: low and insistent. you have, this week, started to be able to track them. you do not, today, look down.

deck.title: aria asks a question
deck.flavor: the second voice. it asks: who am i. or it asks: where is my father. or it asks, today, this is not a body, this is a protocol.

deck.title: sammy's bar runs low on water
deck.flavor: sammy fills the pitcher. you take the pitcher. you carry it to where it has to go.

deck.title: dante in the window
deck.flavor: through the leaded window of the helm. you see his shape behind the glass. he is watching the floor.

deck.title: the catering phone rings
deck.flavor: downstairs · the catering office line · somebody in beaumont wants three hundred plates by october.

deck.title: hector crosses himself
deck.flavor: you see it through the pass. somebody you do not see has, somewhere on the floor, come in.

deck.title: the private dining door
deck.flavor: the partition. closed. inside, the six-top from earlier is in the middle of the third course. you can hear, faintly, the laughter that is not the kind of laughter you have to manage.

deck.title: the bus kid drops a tray
deck.flavor: in the back corridor. the sound carries to the floor. you smooth your apron. you do not, today, turn your head toward it.

deck.title: the river in the window
deck.flavor: from table 17's window, between seatings. the muddy divide between texas and louisiana. you look once, briefly, and look away.

deck.title: dean arrives at table 14
deck.flavor: an out-of-town suit. you seat him at fourteen. he does not, in the next two hours, order.

deck.title: aria speaks · the room registers a half-pause
deck.flavor: the second voice says a thing inside you and your face, for half a beat, does the small unfamiliar thing it does when she is the one driving. the room registers the half-beat. nobody mentions it.

deck.title: the night held
deck.flavor: the floor held. the kitchen kept up. table 17 paid and left. the bus kid did not break a third tray. dante turned the helm light off.
